2605.25566.title: Uncertainty Reasoning with Large Language Models for Explainable Disease Diagnosis
2605.25566.paper_summary: Clinical decision-making requires reasoning over incomplete, imprecise, and linguistically expressed patient narratives. While large language models (LLMs) excel at extracting latent information from natural language, they lack the verifiability and interpretability essential for trustworthy medical AI. We propose a neuro-symbolic reasoning framework that aligns LLMs with formal logic to enable explainable and formally verifiable medical diagnosis. Patient descriptions and clinical guidelines are embedded into a neural knowledge base, where LLMs extract structured medical entities, temporal relations, and fuzzy symptom patterns, which are decoded into a symbolic knowledge base expressed in fuzzy logic and declarative rules. We perform two-stage reasoning: (1) inductive symbolic generalization to capture diagnostic patterns from encoded narratives, and (2) inference verification via a logic programming engine to derive and validate diagnoses consistent with clinical standards. Each symptom is treated as a fuzzy predicate with probabilistic weights, and inference paths are auditable, adjustable, and compatible with physician feedback. Unlike purely statistical methods, our system supports iterative refinement: misalignment between LLM-generated diagnoses and ground truth can be traced, explained, and corrected through formal rules. By combining logic-based transparency, LLM adaptability, and probabilistic robustness, the framework enables human-aligned healthcare inference with strong generalization and verifiable, step-by-step reasoning chains. We validate our framework on public benchmarks, demonstrating effective reconciliation of symbolic reasoning and LLMs with real-world clinical narratives. Results show performance comparable to state-of-the-art LLMs, while additionally providing interpretable reasoning paths and formally verifiable diagnostic conclusions.
2605.25566.paper_summary_zh: 臨床決策需要對不完整、不精確以及以語言表達的病人敘述進行推理。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在從自然語言中提取潛在信息方面表現出色，但它們缺乏可信醫療人工智慧所需的可驗證性和可解釋性。我們提出了一個神經符號推理框架，將LLMs與形式邏輯對齊，以實現可解釋和形式可驗證的醫療診斷。病人描述和臨床指導方針被嵌入到神經知識庫中，LLMs提取結構化的醫療實體、時間關係和模糊症狀模式，這些被解碼為用模糊邏輯和聲明規則表達的符號知識庫。我們執行兩階段推理：（1）歸納符號概括，以從編碼的敘述中捕捉診斷模式，和（2）通過邏輯編程引擎進行推理驗證，以推導和驗證與臨床標準一致的診斷。每個症狀被視為具有概率權重的模糊謂詞，推理路徑是可審計的、可調整的，並且與醫生的反饋相容。與純粹的統計方法不同，我們的系統支持迭代改進：LLM生成的診斷與真實情況之間的不一致可以通過形式規則進行追蹤、解釋和修正。通過結合基於邏輯的透明度、LLM的適應性和概率穩健性，該框架實現了與人類對齊的醫療推理，具有強大的泛化能力和可驗證的逐步推理鏈。我們在公共基準上驗證了我們的框架，展示了符號推理與LLMs在現實臨床敘述中的有效調和。結果顯示，其性能可與最先進的LLMs相媲美，同時提供可解釋的推理路徑和形式可驗證的診斷結論。
2605.25566.author: Xiaoyang Fan et.al.
2605.25566.authors: Xiaoyang Fan, Yufan Cai, Zhe Hou, Jin Song Dong
